100 Years Ago Today, This is What Was Happening ...

On May 17, 1926, the British general strike entered its second week, with labor unions protesting against wage cuts and deteriorating working conditions, causing significant disruption across the nation. In Germany, political tensions escalated as the Reichstag debated reparations and economic recovery in the wake of World War I. Meanwhile, in the United States, the ongoing fight against Prohibition continued to ignite debates over morality and law enforcement.
Updated: Sunday, May 17, 2026 at 07:45:08 AM CDT
